results.title: Water reaches its real density
results.why: SPC/E water at 300 K, 1 bar. The reference is the WATER MODEL's own published density (998), not experiment (997) — reproducing experiment is a property of SPC/E, not of our code. What this tests is whether WE set the simulation up correctly. The old window (975–1025) was wide enough to accept TIP3P (986.0 ± 1.3, measured) as well as SPC/E (999.7 ± 1.1, measured) — the two are 8σ apart, so it could not tell a correct setup from one running the wrong water model. It passed for the wrong reason.

results.title: Water's hydrogen-bond shell is in the right place
results.why: The first peak of the O–O radial distribution function is at 0.28 nm in real water (neutron scattering). This is the structure of liquid water itself.

results.title: The thermostat actually holds the temperature
results.why: Mean temperature must equal the setpoint. If it drifts, the integrator or coupling is broken and every result downstream is garbage.

results.title: Liquid argon is structured
results.why: A dense liquid has a strong first coordination shell: g(r) peaks well above 2. If this is flat, we are not simulating a liquid.

results.title: Argon gas is NOT structured
results.why: The same code at low density must give a nearly flat g(r). This is the control: it proves the previous test measured physics, not an artefact.

results.title: Quantum energy of water matches the textbook
results.why: Hartree–Fock/STO-3G on water is a canonical number: −74.96 Hartree. Any competent QM code reproduces it exactly.

results.title: The O–H bond comes out the right length
results.why: Scanning the bond must put the energy minimum at the real O–H distance (~0.96 Å). This validates the whole reaction-profile machinery.

results.title: A stable protein stays folded
results.why: Ubiquitin is a rigid, well-behaved protein. Backbone RMSD must plateau low (< 0.4 nm). A big number means the force field, ions or box are wrong — or it unfolded, which it should not.

uncertainty.note: REFUSED: n_eff=19.7 (need >=25), 2 blocking level(s) (need >=4). The run is too short relative to its own correlation time (tau_int=0.7 samples) for ANY defensible error bar.
